title: Corallocarpus courbonii
taxonomy: Corallocarpus courbonii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cucurbitaceae. This family includes a wide range of plants commonly known as gourds and melons. The genus Corallocarpus is a small group of plants native to Africa, with Corallocarpus courbonii being one of its species. Corallocarpus courbonii is further classified as a member of the Cucurbitaceae subfamily Cucurbitoideae. The plant's classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other members of the Cucurbitaceae family.

This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties.
